response:
The author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. They want to calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards dealt. This can be approached using combinatorial functions. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from 52 is $ \binom{52}{5} $. The number of ways to choose 5 cards that do not contain any hearts is $ \binom{39}{5} $, since there are 39 non-heart cards. The probability of getting at least one heart is then:

$$
1 - \frac{\binom{39}{5}}{\binom{52}{5}}
$$

This expression represents the probability of at least one heart in the next 5 cards, expressed in terms of combinatorial functions.

*** 

The author is faced with a choice between two strategies. Strategy A has an expected value of $ 0.4 \times 1000 - 0.6 \times 500 = 400 - 300 = 100 $. Strategy B has an expected value of $ 0.6 \times 800 - 0.4 \times 200 = 480 - 80 = 400 $. The author should choose Strategy B, as it offers a higher expected value. 

Bag of cards, bag of decisions, bag of choices, bag of luck—each bag holds a different kind of value. Bag of risks, bag of rewards, bag of outcomes, bag of probabilities. Bag of all, bag of everything, bag of life, bag of destiny.
